If the cage itself is cracked, you can usually replace just the cage. Or you might consider upgrading to dual-sided pedals — SPD cleats on one side, flat platform on the other. They're about thirty to fifty dollars and a big upgrade.

steps.question: What kind of cleats does your bike use — do you know? Peloton uses Look Delta, which are the large triangle-shaped cleats. Most gym spin bikes use SPD, which are smaller and recessed into the shoe.
steps.if_look_delta: Check the cleats on the bottom of your shoes. Look Delta cleats wear out and the tips get rounded. If the red, yellow, or gray tips are worn down, they won't clip in securely. Replacement cleats are about fifteen to twenty-five dollars and you just need an Allen wrench to swap them.
steps.if_spd: SPD cleats also wear out but they're very cheap — about ten to fifteen dollars for a set. Check if the cleat is loose on the shoe, too. The two bolts can vibrate loose over time.
steps.if_not_sure: Look at the bottom of your cycling shoe. If you see a large triangle shape, that's Look Delta. If you see a small rectangular metal piece recessed into the sole, that's SPD.

steps.question: How old are your cleats? If you've been riding regularly for more than six months, they're probably due for a replacement regardless.
steps.if_old: Fresh cleats will make a world of difference. They'll clip in and out crisply. It's one of those things where you don't realize how worn they are until you put new ones on.
steps.if_new_or_recent: If the cleats are new and still won't clip in, the pedal mechanism itself might be worn or damaged. Try a different pair of shoes if you can, to rule out the cleat. If it's the pedal, replacement pedals are about thirty to one hundred dollars depending on the type, and they just thread on.

steps.question: Sound like something you can handle, or want our tech to take a look?

spin_bike__spin_bike_pedal_or_cleat_prob.resolution_needs_tech: Let me grab your info. Our tech will check the pedals, cleats, and crank arms and make sure everything is working properly.
spin_bike__spin_bike_pedal_or_cleat_prob.resolution_diy: Cleats and pedals are something most people can handle at home. Just remember — left pedal is reverse-threaded. And keep those cleat bolts checked periodically. Call us if you need anything else.
spin_bike__spin_bike_pedal_or_cleat_prob.safety_abort: If a pedal is wobbly or feels like it might come off during riding, stop immediately. A pedal failure while standing and sprinting can cause a serious fall. Tighten it or replace it before riding again.

cable_machine_cable_fraying.symptom: Visible wire strands poking out of cable, rough feel when pulling cable, cable appears fuzzy or thinning at any point
cable_machine_cable_fraying.root_cause: Fatigue failure from repeated bending cycles around pulleys, sweat corrosion, pulley misalignment causing uneven wear, or age (manufacturers recommend replacement every 2-3 years)
cable_machine_cable_fraying.fix_cost: $30-80 cable DIY, $199 flat rate at many shops, $150-300 with labor

cable_machine_weight_stack_selector_pin.symptom: Pin won't fully engage, weight plate slides off during lift, pin bent or won't slide in, clicking noise from weight stack
cable_machine_weight_stack_selector_pin.root_cause: Pin bending from being dropped or forced at an angle, guide hole wear, or debris in the pin channel

cable_machine_frame_hardware.symptom: Wobbling or instability during use, clicking from frame joints, visible gap between frame members, bolts found on floor
cable_machine_frame_hardware.root_cause: Hardware loosening from vibration over time, especially on bolted (non-welded) frame connections

cable_machine_attachment_carabiner_swive.symptom: Carabiner gate won't close fully, swivel seized and won't rotate, attachment disconnects during use
cable_machine_attachment_carabiner_swive.root_cause: Spring fatigue in carabiner gate, sweat corrosion seizing swivel, or worn latch mechanism

cable_machine_cable_termination_ferrule.symptom: Cable pulling out of end fitting, loose ball/stop at cable end, cable end fraying near the ferrule
cable_machine_cable_termination_ferrule.root_cause: Crimped ferrule loosening from repeated stress cycles, or corrosion weakening the cable strands at the ferrule junction

cable_machine_pulley_bearings.symptom: Grinding or squeaking noise during cable movement, jerky cable motion, cable jumping off pulley track, pulley not spinning freely
cable_machine_pulley_bearings.root_cause: Bearing wear from lack of lubrication, dust/dirt contamination, or moisture from sweat and humidity
cable_machine_pulley_bearings.fix_cost: $10-30 bearings DIY, $80-150 with labor per pulley

cable_machine_cable_routing.symptom: Cable jumped off pulley, cable rubbing on frame, uneven resistance through range of motion, cable crossed over itself
cable_machine_cable_routing.root_cause: Cable jumped its track from sudden release of tension (dropping weight stack), worn pulley groove, or improper cable replacement

cable_machine_weight_stack_guide_rods.symptom: Weight stack sticking during lift, grinding noise from weight stack, uneven plate movement, plates tilting
cable_machine_weight_stack_guide_rods.root_cause: Guide rod corrosion from sweat and humidity, lack of lubrication, bent guide rod from impact, or guide bushing wear in weight plates
cable_machine_weight_stack_guide_rods.fix_cost: $0 (lubricate), $30-60 bushings, $80-200 guide rod replacement with labor

elliptical_stride_arm_bearings.symptom: Squeaking, clicking, or popping sounds during stride, especially at top or bottom of motion. Noise worsens over time
elliptical_stride_arm_bearings.root_cause: Sealed bearing wear in pivot points of stride/linkage arms from repetitive motion and sweat exposure

elliptical_resistance_motor_servo.symptom: Resistance won't change when adjusted, resistance stuck at one level, no resistance at all, motor buzzing but resistance not responding
elliptical_resistance_motor_servo.root_cause: Resistance motor (servo or eddy current brake) failure, wiring harness damage, or control board signal loss

elliptical_pedal_bearings.symptom: Clicking or grinding noise from pedal area during each stride, pedal feels loose or wobbly, lateral play in pedal
elliptical_pedal_bearings.root_cause: Pedal arm bearing wear, often accelerated by user weight and lateral forces during exercise

elliptical_crank_arm_and_axle.symptom: Loud clunk once per revolution, visible wobble in crank arm, pedal arm feels like it shifts laterally during stride
elliptical_crank_arm_and_axle.root_cause: Crank arm bolt loosening over time, worn splines on crank arm or axle, or bent axle from impact
elliptical_crank_arm_and_axle.fix_cost: $0 (retorque bolt), $40-120 crank arm, $80-200 axle with labor

elliptical_console_display.symptom: Console completely dead, display flickers, buttons unresponsive, console powers on but shows no data
elliptical_console_display.root_cause: Failed power supply to console, loose wiring harness between frame and console mast, or internal board failure

elliptical_flywheel_bearings.symptom: Grinding, humming, or whirring noise that increases with pedal speed, coming from the flywheel housing area
elliptical_flywheel_bearings.root_cause: Flywheel shaft bearing wear from age and dust contamination

elliptical_drive_belt.symptom: Slipping sensation during stride, squealing noise from flywheel area, visible cracking or fraying on belt
elliptical_drive_belt.root_cause: Drive belt wear from normal use, belt stretching beyond adjustment range, or belt glazing from heat

elliptical_roller_wheels.symptom: Thumping or bumping sensation during stride, visible flat spots on guide rollers, plastic debris near roller tracks
elliptical_roller_wheels.root_cause: Guide roller wheels developing flat spots from sitting in one position for extended periods, or general wear
elliptical_roller_wheels.fix_cost: $30-60 per wheel set DIY, $120-200 with labor

elliptical_stride_length_mechanism.symptom: Stride length stuck at one setting, power stride adjustment motor not responding, clunking when stride changes
elliptical_stride_length_mechanism.root_cause: Motorized stride mechanism seized, track rails corroded or debris-bound, or adjustment motor failure

exercise_bike_resistance_system.symptom: No resistance at any level, resistance stuck at one level, uneven resistance during pedaling, squealing when resistance is applied
exercise_bike_resistance_system.root_cause: Magnetic brake misalignment, friction pad wear (felt/leather), eddy current brake wiring failure, or resistance control cable stretch
exercise_bike_resistance_system.fix_cost: $15-40 pads/cable DIY, $50-150 magnetic system, $100-250 with labor

exercise_bike_seat_post_adjustment.symptom: Seat drops during riding, adjustment knob won't hold position, seat post stuck and won't adjust, creaking from seat area
exercise_bike_seat_post_adjustment.root_cause: Quick-release or pop-pin mechanism wear, corrosion on seat post from sweat, stripped adjustment threading

exercise_bike_pedal_bearings.symptom: Clicking once per revolution, grinding from pedal area, pedal wobbles on spindle, pedal loosening during use
exercise_bike_pedal_bearings.root_cause: Pedal bearing wear, loose pedal threads (note: left pedal is reverse-threaded), or worn crank arm threads
exercise_bike_pedal_bearings.fix_cost: $15-50 replacement pedals DIY, $30-100 for SPD/clipless, $80-150 with labor

exercise_bike_crank_arm.symptom: Clunking once per pedal revolution, visible movement at crank-to-axle junction, pedal arm feels like it shifts
exercise_bike_crank_arm.root_cause: Crank arm bolt loosening, stripped splines on crank arm or bottom bracket axle, or crank arm fatigue crack
exercise_bike_crank_arm.fix_cost: $20-50 crank arm DIY, $40-80 with crank puller tool, $100-180 with labor

exercise_bike_flywheel_bearings.symptom: Grinding or whirring noise that increases with pedal speed, rough/gritty feel when pedaling, vibration through frame
exercise_bike_flywheel_bearings.root_cause: Flywheel shaft bearing wear from age, dust infiltration, or moisture from sweat

exercise_bike_drive_belt_chain.symptom: Slipping under hard pedaling, visible cracks or missing teeth on belt, chain noise or skipping, loss of power transfer
exercise_bike_drive_belt_chain.root_cause: Belt stretch or wear (belt-drive), chain stretch or rust (chain-drive), or tension adjustment needed
exercise_bike_drive_belt_chain.fix_cost: $20-50 belt or chain DIY, $80-150 with labor

exercise_bike_bottom_bracket_bearings.symptom: Creaking or clicking centered at the crank area (not pedals), rough rotation when cranks are spun by hand, lateral play in crank axle
exercise_bike_bottom_bracket_bearings.root_cause: Bottom bracket bearing wear from sweat dripping down seat post into bracket area, dust, and normal use

exercise_bike_console_display.symptom: Console dead, no display, inaccurate readings, buttons unresponsive, Bluetooth/ANT+ connectivity lost
exercise_bike_console_display.root_cause: Dead batteries (many bikes use AA or AAA), loose wiring, reed switch misalignment (for RPM/speed), or board failure

rower_handle_cord_chain.symptom: Chain skipping or running roughly, cord fraying visually, handle doesn't retract smoothly, snapping noise during pull
rower_handle_cord_chain.root_cause: Chain link stiffness from lack of lubrication, cord wear from repeated bending, or sprocket wear

rower_resistance_mechanism.symptom: No resistance or inconsistent resistance, gritty feel during pull, damper not responding to setting changes
rower_resistance_mechanism.root_cause: Water rowers: impeller vane damage or water level change. Air rowers: damper flap stuck or dirty. Magnetic: servo motor failure or wiring issue
rower_resistance_mechanism.fix_cost: $0 (cleaning/adjustment), $30-80 damper parts, $80-200 servo motor, $100-300 with labor

rower_seat_rollers_rail.symptom: Seat sticks or jerks during rowing, rough sliding motion, wobbling seat, grinding noise from seat area
rower_seat_rollers_rail.root_cause: Worn seat rollers developing flat spots, dirty/debris-covered rail, or bent monorail from impact

rower_flywheel_clutch.symptom: Handle pulls but no resistance generated, flywheel spins freely in both directions, slipping feel during the drive phase
rower_flywheel_clutch.root_cause: One-way clutch/bearing failure preventing flywheel engagement, contaminated or dried-out clutch lubricant

rower_water_tank_seal.symptom: Water leaking from tank, water level dropping without visible leak, algae or discoloration in water
rower_water_tank_seal.root_cause: Tank seal degradation, stopper plug failure, or micro-cracks in polycarbonate tank from UV exposure or age

rower_monitor_display.symptom: Monitor dead or intermittent, display segments missing, inaccurate stroke count or distance, monitor resets during rowing
rower_monitor_display.root_cause: Battery terminal corrosion from sweat exposure (60% of display failures), failed pickup sensor, or internal board damage

rower_foot_strap_plate.symptom: Foot strap won't hold tension, strap buckle broken, foot plate cracked, heel cup broken
rower_foot_strap_plate.root_cause: Strap material degradation from sweat and UV, buckle mechanism wear, or foot plate fatigue cracking from repeated flex
rower_foot_strap_plate.fix_cost: $10-25 straps DIY, $20-50 foot plates, $60-100 with labor

rower_bungee_shock_cord.symptom: Handle doesn't retract fully, handle hangs loose at full extension, slow retraction speed
rower_bungee_shock_cord.root_cause: Bungee/shock cord stretching from repeated use and age, or cold temperature reducing elasticity

smith_machine_safety_catch_mechanism.symptom: Safety catches won't engage at some positions, bar doesn't lock when rotated, catches feel loose or don't hold under load
smith_machine_safety_catch_mechanism.root_cause: Safety hook wear from repeated engagement, spring fatigue in auto-locking mechanisms, or debris in catch slots

smith_machine_cable_pulley_system.symptom: Cable fraying, pulley grinding, uneven cable pull, cable jumped off track
smith_machine_cable_pulley_system.root_cause: Same failure modes as standalone cable machines — cable fatigue, pulley bearing wear, and routing issues

smith_machine_frame_bolts.symptom: Wobbling or racking movement, clicking sounds from frame joints, visible gaps at bolt junctions, bolts found on floor
smith_machine_frame_bolts.root_cause: Bolt loosening from vibration and repeated loading/unloading cycles, especially on bolt-together frames

smith_machine_j_hook_safeties.symptom: J-hooks won't stay at set height, safeties drop under load, adjustment pin bent or missing
smith_machine_j_hook_safeties.root_cause: Pop-pin spring fatigue, hole enlargement in uprights from repeated use, or j-hook face wear

smith_machine_linear_bearings.symptom: Bar sticks or grinds during movement, bar doesn't slide smoothly, visible scoring on guide rods, uneven feel between sides
smith_machine_linear_bearings.root_cause: Linear bearing wear from lack of lubrication, dust and chalk contamination, or guide rod surface damage
smith_machine_linear_bearings.fix_cost: $20-60 bearings DIY, $50-120 with lubricant and bearings, $150-300 with labor

smith_machine_bar_rotation_mechanism.symptom: Bar won't rotate smoothly for hook engagement, inconsistent lock/unlock feel, bar gets stuck in locked position
smith_machine_bar_rotation_mechanism.root_cause: Rotation bushing wear, debris in rotation channel, or hook geometry worn from repeated use

smith_machine_weight_plate_pegs.symptom: Plates won't slide on/off easily, peg bent or wobbling, weight plates shifting during movement
smith_machine_weight_plate_pegs.root_cause: Peg bending from uneven loading or impact, weld point fatigue where peg meets frame, or plate hole burrs catching

smith_machine_counter_balance_system.symptom: Bar feels excessively heavy at starting position, uneven bar weight through range, gas strut leaking, springs losing tension
smith_machine_counter_balance_system.root_cause: Gas strut seal failure causing pressure loss, spring fatigue from years of cycling, or counter-balance cable wear

spin_bike_resistance_pad_brake.symptom: Squealing noise when resistance applied, uneven braking feel, resistance knob turns but no change in difficulty, burning smell
spin_bike_resistance_pad_brake.root_cause: Felt/leather brake pad wear from friction against flywheel, pad glazing, or pad holder misalignment

spin_bike_flywheel_bearings.symptom: Grinding, clicking, or rubbing noise that gets louder with speed, rough/gritty feel when pedaling, vibration through frame
spin_bike_flywheel_bearings.root_cause: Bearing wear accelerated by sweat exposure (sweat drips directly onto flywheel housing on many spin bikes), dust, and high-RPM stress

spin_bike_pedal_cleat_mechanism.symptom: Cleat won't clip in or out, pedal feels loose when clipped in, clicking during pedal stroke, toe cage straps broken
spin_bike_pedal_cleat_mechanism.root_cause: Cleat mechanism wear (SPD, Look Delta, or toe cage), pedal bearing failure, or cleat bolts loosened on shoe
spin_bike_pedal_cleat_mechanism.fix_cost: $15-50 pedals DIY (toe cage), $30-100 clipless pedals, $80-150 with labor

spin_bike_seat_post_adjustment.symptom: Seat drops during riding, seat won't stay at set height, seat fore/aft adjustment slips, clicking from seat area
spin_bike_seat_post_adjustment.root_cause: Same corrosion and clamp issues as handlebar — worsened by direct sweat contact from seated position

spin_bike_sweat_damage_electronics.symptom: Console intermittently dies, Bluetooth/WiFi connectivity lost, touchscreen unresponsive in some areas, corrosion visible on frame near electronics
spin_bike_sweat_damage_electronics.root_cause: Sweat is corrosive (salt + moisture) and spin bikes generate extreme sweat volumes that drip directly onto the frame, electronics, and internal components

spin_bike_handlebar_adjustment.symptom: Handlebar drops during riding, height adjustment won't hold, handlebar wobbles side to side, creaking during climbing position
spin_bike_handlebar_adjustment.root_cause: Pop-pin mechanism wear, knob clamp insufficient pressure, handlebar post corrosion from sweat, or stripped adjustment threads

spin_bike_drive_belt_chain.symptom: Clicking or ticking noise at regular intervals, slipping under hard effort, visible wear or cracks on belt, chain noise or rust
spin_bike_drive_belt_chain.root_cause: Belt stretch over time, missing belt teeth, chain stretch from lack of lubrication, or tension adjustment needed
spin_bike_drive_belt_chain.fix_cost: $20-50 belt or chain DIY, $80-150 with labor

spin_bike_bottom_bracket_bearings.symptom: Creaking or clicking centered at crank junction, rough pedal rotation when spun by hand, lateral wobble in crank arms
spin_bike_bottom_bracket_bearings.root_cause: Bottom bracket bearing wear from sweat dripping down seat post into bracket area, high-torque spinning loads

stair_stepper_step_chain.symptom: Clicking or grinding noise during stepping, chain visibly slack, steps feel uneven or jerky, chain jumping sprockets
stair_stepper_step_chain.root_cause: Chain stretch from normal use (commercial revolving staircase units), worn sprockets, or insufficient lubrication
stair_stepper_step_chain.fix_cost: $100-300 chain, $200-500 with labor. Sprocket replacement additional $100-200

stair_stepper_alternator_generator.symptom: No resistance regardless of level setting, resistance fluctuates unpredictably, burning smell from alternator housing
stair_stepper_alternator_generator.root_cause: Alternator brush wear (similar to treadmill motor brushes), bearing failure, or winding damage in the resistance generator
stair_stepper_alternator_generator.fix_cost: $30-60 brush kit DIY, $150-400 alternator replacement with labor

stair_stepper_hydraulic_cylinder.symptom: One or both pedals sink without resistance, oil visible around cylinder, pedal won't return to top position
stair_stepper_hydraulic_cylinder.root_cause: Hydraulic cylinder seal failure allowing fluid leak, loss of hydraulic fluid pressure

stair_stepper_step_height_calibration.symptom: Steps don't reach full range of motion, bottom step hits frame, uneven step depth between left and right
stair_stepper_step_height_calibration.root_cause: Calibration drift from chain stretch, sensor misalignment, or control board losing stored calibration values

stair_stepper_drive_belt.symptom: Slipping sensation under load, squealing from drive area, reduced step resistance, visible belt wear
stair_stepper_drive_belt.root_cause: Drive belt wear from normal use, belt tension loss, or pulley misalignment

stair_stepper_step_rod_bearings.symptom: Grinding noise from step mechanism, rough step motion, visible rust on step rods
stair_stepper_step_rod_bearings.root_cause: Bearing wear and corrosion from sweat exposure, lack of lubrication schedule adherence

stair_stepper_pedal_pivot_bushings.symptom: Lateral wobble in pedals/steps, clunking sound at top or bottom of step stroke, visible play when pedal is pushed side to side
stair_stepper_pedal_pivot_bushings.root_cause: Bushing wear from repetitive motion, especially on independent-pedal stepper designs

stair_stepper_console_display.symptom: Console dead, display shows garbled data, step count inaccurate, buttons unresponsive
stair_stepper_console_display.root_cause: Wiring harness damage from vibration, battery corrosion (battery-powered consoles), or main board failure

treadmill_walking_belt_and_deck.symptom: Belt slows or stops under load, hesitates when stepped on, burning smell from friction
treadmill_walking_belt_and_deck.root_cause: Walking belt and/or deck surface worn out, creating excess friction. Accounts for 85% of treadmill power-loss complaints per Treadmill Doctor data

treadmill_power_cord_inlet.symptom: Treadmill completely dead, intermittent power cuts especially when cord is moved, visible damage or burn marks at power inlet
treadmill_power_cord_inlet.root_cause: Power cord internal break from repeated flexing (especially if treadmill is moved), or inlet receptacle solder joints cracked from vibration

treadmill_motor_brushes.symptom: Burning smell at higher speeds, motor sparking visible through vents, intermittent power loss, treadmill struggles under load
treadmill_motor_brushes.root_cause: Carbon brush wear from normal use, accelerated by dust buildup. Brushes are consumable contact points inside DC motors

treadmill_belt_tension_and_tracking.symptom: Belt slips when running or walking, belt drifts left or right, belt feels loose underfoot
treadmill_belt_tension_and_tracking.root_cause: Belt tension too loose (stretches over time) or rear roller misaligned causing drift. Over-tightening causes premature bearing and motor wear

treadmill_safety_key_switch.symptom: Treadmill won't start at all, intermittently shuts off mid-use, safety key appears properly seated but no response
treadmill_safety_key_switch.root_cause: Reed switch or magnetic switch behind safety key slot worn or failed, safety key magnet weakened, or corroded contacts
treadmill_safety_key_switch.fix_cost: $5-15 DIY (replacement key), $10-30 for switch, $80-120 with labor

treadmill_motor_controller_board.symptom: Treadmill powers on but belt won't move, erratic speed changes, complete power failure, burning electronics smell, error codes on display
treadmill_motor_controller_board.root_cause: Power surge damage, capacitor failure, MOSFET burnout, or loose wire connections on the MCB. Heat buildup from dust accelerates failure

treadmill_incline_motor.symptom: Incline runs up/down continuously without stopping, incline won't respond to controls, incline calibration error codes, grinding noise during incline change
treadmill_incline_motor.root_cause: Incline motor burnout, damaged wiring between deck and lift motor, or calibration drift requiring recalibration procedure

treadmill_console_display.symptom: Console completely dead, display flickers or is unresponsive, touchscreen frozen, iFIT software errors or retry loops
treadmill_console_display.root_cause: Loose wiring between console and controller, failed power board inside console, software corruption (iFIT-equipped models), or blown internal fuse

treadmill_speed_sensor.symptom: Erratic speed readings, speed display jumps randomly, E05 or similar error codes, console shows speed but belt isn't matching
treadmill_speed_sensor.root_cause: Speed sensor (reed switch or optical) misaligned from front roller magnet, sensor failure, or debris between sensor and magnet

treadmill_drive_belt.symptom: Squealing noise from motor area, belt slips at higher speeds or under load, visible cracking or glazing on belt surface
treadmill_drive_belt.root_cause: Drive belt (connects motor to front roller) stretching, glazing, or cracking from heat and age

treadmill_roller_bearings.symptom: Grinding or rumbling noise from front or rear roller area, vibration felt through deck, belt tracking issues that won't resolve with adjustment
treadmill_roller_bearings.root_cause: Bearing wear from age, dust infiltration, or lack of lubrication. Front roller bearings fail more often due to motor drive stress
treadmill_roller_bearings.fix_cost: $20-50 bearings DIY, $80-200 roller assembly, $150-300 with labor

safety_rules: Burning smell or sparking: UNPLUG IMMEDIATELY, schedule tech, do not troubleshoot further
Exposed wiring: UNPLUG, tech visit only
Machine making grinding metal sounds: STOP USING, could cause injury
Treadmill belt catching/grabbing: STOP USING, fall hazard
Heavy equipment tilting or unstable: STOP USING, secure and call tech

scheduling_info.talk_track_az: Our tech is in the Arizona area during the week. What day works best - any weekday preference?
scheduling_info.talk_track_ca: Our tech covers Southern California on weekends. Would Saturday or Sunday work better for you?
scheduling_info.talk_track_flexible: Let me note that preference and we'll see what we can work out.
scheduling_info.do_not_promise: Never confirm a specific appointment. Say: 'I'll pass this along and someone will confirm your appointment shortly.'

Clicking or grinding noise during stepping, chain visibly slack, steps feel uneven or jerky, chain jumping sprockets

common_caller_scenarios.cost_range: $15-40 pads/cable DIY, $50-150 magnetic system, $100-250 with labor

Visible wire strands poking out of cable, rough feel when pulling cable, cable appears fuzzy or thinning at any point
